name: Barbara's Milk Chocolate Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the almonds in a shallow pan in a preheated 350°F oven for 12 to 15 minutes, shaking the pan once or twice. Remove and set aside until cool.
2. Break chocolate into small pieces and place it in the bowl of a food processor fitted with the metal chopping blade. Let stand.
3. Sift together flour, baking soda, salt, cocoa powder, and sugar. Add about 1 cup of the sifted mixture to the chocolate. Process for 30 seconds or until the chocolate is fine and powdery.
4. In a large bowl, mix processed ingredients with remaining sifted dry ingredients. Stir in almonds.
5. Beat eggs with vanilla just to mix. Stir egg mixture into dry ingredients with a rubber or wooden spatula.
6. Place two 20-inch lengths of plastic wrap on work surface.
7. Shape dough: Spoon a strip of dough down the middle of each piece of plastic wrap, about 13-14 inches long. Flatten tops. Lift long sides of plastic wrap and press to shape into an even strip (14-15 inches long, 2½-3 inches wide, ¾ inch thick).
8. Freeze strips for at least 2 hours or until firm.
9. Preheat oven to 300°F. Line two large cookie sheets with parchment paper or aluminum foil (shiny side up).
10. Transfer frozen dough strips onto lined sheets, upside down. Peel off plastic wrap. Repeat with second strip and sheet.
11. Bake for 1 hour, rotating sheets top-to-bottom and front-to-back once to ensure even baking. Strips will spread to about 7-8 inches wide during baking.
12. Reduce oven temperature to 275°F. Using spatula, release a strip from parchment/foil onto a board. Repeat with second strip.
13. Cut strips crosswise into slices ½-¾ inch thick using a serrated French bread knife. Place slices upright on unlined cookie sheets with space between.
14. Return to oven and bake at 275°F for an additional 40 minutes, rotating sheets top-to-bottom and front-to-back once more.
15. Cool completely and store in an airtight container.
Per biscotto: 80 calories, 2 gm protein, 11 gm carbohydrates, 4 gm fat, 17 mg cholesterol, 1 gm saturated fat, 41 mg sodium
The dough requires about 2 hours of freezing before baking. Two bakings total approximately 1 hour and 40 minutes.
